title: Trying to build an autonomous trading system for Indian markets
author: Tight-Pepper-4721
selftext:
Over the last few weeks I’ve been building an autonomous AI-driven trading system for Indian markets.  
Not a signals group.  
Not a “ChatGPT predicts stocks” gimmick either.  
More like an attempt to build a real autonomous decision-making system where AI thinks, Python controls execution/risk, and the whole thing can survive real-world failure conditions without completely losing its mind.

What started as curiosity honestly turned into a pretty deep obsession.  
The interesting part hasn’t even been the trading itself — it’s been solving problems around hallucinations, overtrading, broker failures, etc. and trying to figure out how an AI system should behave in chaotic market conditions.  
Still early.  
Still paper trading.  
Still experimenting.  
.  
Posting here because I’m curious if other people are building/exploring similar things around:  
	•	AI agents  
	•	quant systems  
	•	autonomous infrastructure  
	•	trading architecture  
 Etc

Would genuinely love to connect with people thinking deeply in this space.  
Especially people more interested in building something similar.

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body:
You are on the right track. It’s definitely the future. 

I’m part of a team that built and building a platform where you house ai traders , that work in multi agentic flows. It’s currently doing forex major pairs and US indexes, via CFD’s. 

The platform shows all of the work from análisis to trade execution decision. 

It’s called SkyAnalyst AI. Check it out. It may give you ideas how to approach the problem .

replies.author: Tight-Pepper-4721
replies.body:
Sure I’ll check it out , thanks   
Also would love to connect with you and get some advise

comments.author: Annual-Register-3683
comments.body:
this is honestly a really interesting direction. Most people stop at basic bots or indicators but trying to build something that can actually handle bad market conditions, execution issues and its own mistakes is a completely different level

the part about broker failures and overtrading caught my attention because that’s the stuff people usually ignore until it blows up a system live. Building the logic is one thing, building safeguards around it is probably the harder part

I’ve been exploring smaller automation projects too and one thing I realized fast is how important stable infrastructure becomes once you let systems run on their own. I keep most of my testing environment on a separate setup through tradingfx vps so things stay running without needing constant supervision

comments.author: Prior-Egg-3099
comments.body:
i have a similar thing built:

you add stock ticker to your watchlist.(runs on discord)
ai analyses stocks on watchlist from all users.
5 different bots trade the assets and generate alerts when trade starts or closes. (if user activates alerts)

its all on paper still ( except onchain bot) and maybe you add your holdings with buy price or  you just try to outperform the bots trading your wishlist asset on your own broker.
( no fin advice, more a trading simulator with live data )

i have a terminal too, not yet public where you can see all the trades and p/l incl. some hypothetical fees paid best trades and a lot more.

not sure yet if i want to make it public or not x) im not a sales person :)

comments.author: bmbybrew
comments.body:
"decision-making system where AI thinks"  
What part of system or workflow is rule based v/s traditional machine learning v/s thinking using an LLM?

Can you share some examples of what you are thinking?

replies.author: Tight-Pepper-4721
replies.body:
Every part is rule based   
So the LLM has been provided a set of rules and within that it is free to use its brain and place orders and decisions

comments.author: ksraj1001
comments.body: Would be interested if you share more details on this, let’s connect

comments.author: Traditional-Key3091
comments.body: uilding an autonomous trading system is tough, especially in the Indian markets. focus on risk management first. a system's great, but if you can't control your drawdowns, it won't matter. be prepared for the emotional side too; automation can lull you into false security.

replies.author: Tight-Pepper-4721
replies.body: Yes I’m going strong on risk management and capital protection cause I feel also that’s the fundamental step in this

comments.author: New-Owl5212
comments.body:
This resonates a lot — I've been building 

in a similar direction.



My approach has been slightly different:

Rather than full autonomy, I've focused on 

AI as the analysis layer with human execution.



Problems I've solved that you might find 

relevant:



Hallucination control:

→ Structured prompts with strict output format

→ Pre-calculated metrics fed to AI as context

→ AI validates not generates the numbers



Broker failure handling:

→ MT5 connection health checks every cycle

→ Auto reconnect logic with Telegram alert

→ Graceful degradation — system pauses 

   rather than trades blind



Chaotic market conditions:

→ Volatility filter before any signal fires

→ Session-based trading windows only

→ News event detection via calendar API



Still evolving but these have made the 

system significantly more reliable in 

live conditions.



Would genuinely enjoy connecting — 

sounds like we're solving complementary 

problems. DM me. 🚀
